content.text: System shall open a right-side panel when the user clicks the Material Strength Verification test from the Rules and Action → Testing list.
a configuration panel to open when I select the Material Strength Verification test
I can review the evaluation model.
I am viewing the Rules and Action → Testing list,
I click the Material Strength Verification test,
the system opens a right-side evaluation panel.
System shall display the panel title “Material Strength Verification — Evaluation Model”.
the panel title displayed at the top
I know which evaluation model I am viewing.
the system displays the title “Material Strength Verification — Evaluation Model”.
System shall display the Local scope indicator beside the test title.
see the Local scope indicator beside the test title
I know the scope of the rule.
the system shows the Local scope indicator.
System shall display a description “Verification of yield and tensile strength against design assumptions.”
a description explaining the test purpose
I understand what the evaluation verifies.
the system displays the text “Verification of yield and tensile strength against design assumptions.”
System shall display the Strength Ratio Calculation section.
the Strength Ratio Calculation section displayed
I understand how the evaluation is performed.
the system displays the Strength Ratio Calculation section.
System shall display the formula SR = fy_test / fy_design for calculating strength ratio.
I understand how the ratio is calculated.
the Strength Ratio Calculation section is visible,
the system shows the formula SR = fy_test / fy_design.
System shall display information stating that design yield strength is derived from OEM specifications.
information about the source of design yield strength
I know where the reference value originates.
the Strength Ratio Calculation section is displayed,
the system indicates that design yield strength is derived from OEM specifications.
System shall display information indicating that Tested yield strength may be obtained from MTC, hardness conversion, or laboratory tests.
see possible sources of tested yield strength
I understand how the measured value is obtained.
the Strength Ratio Calculation section is displayed,
the system indicates that tested yield strength may come from MTC, hardness conversion, or laboratory tests.
System shall display the Yield Strength Acceptance section defining acceptance criteria.
the Yield Strength Acceptance section displayed
I can review the severity criteria.
the system shows the Yield Strength Acceptance section.

10.title: Define Green Severity for Yield Strength

content.text: System shall define Green severity when SR ≥ 1.00.
the green severity condition defined for yield strength
I know when strength meets design requirements.
yield strength acceptance thresholds are displayed,
the system defines it as SR ≥ 1.00.

11.title: Define Amber Severity for Yield Strength

content.text: System shall define Amber severity when 0.95 ≤ SR < 1.00.
the Amber severity condition defined for yield strength
I know when strength slightly deviates from design assumptions.
yield strength acceptance thresholds are displayed,
the system defines it as 0.95 ≤ SR < 1.00.

12.title: Define Red Severity for Yield Strength

content.text: System shall define Red severity when SR < 0.95.
the Red severity condition defined for yield strength
I know when the material strength is insufficient.
yield strength acceptance thresholds are displayed,
the system defines it as SR < 0.95.
System shall display the Tensile Strength Verification section when tensile strength values are specified.
the Tensile Strength Verification section displayed when applicable
I can evaluate tensile strength results.
the system displays the Tensile Strength Verification section.
System shall mark tensile strength as acceptable when fu_test / fu_design ≥ 0.95. -> Acceptable
I know when tensile strength is acceptable.
the system marks the result as acceptable when fu_test / fu_design ≥ 0.95.

15.title: Define Red Severity for Low Tensile Strength

content.text: System shall mark the test as Red severity when the tensile strength ratio is below 0.95.
the system to mark Red severity for insufficient tensile strength
the system marks the test severity as Red.
System shall display the Pending Verification Rule section.
the Pending Verification Rule section displayed
the system displays the Pending Verification Rule section.

17.title: Mark Verification Pending Without Material Certificate

content.text: System shall mark verification as Pending when no material certificate is provided.
verification marked as Pending when a material certificate is unavailable
the system marks the verification as Pending.

18.title: Mark Verification Pending Without Material Test

content.text: System shall mark verification as Pending when no material test has been performed.
verification marked as Pending when no test has been performed
no material test has been performed,
the system marks the verification as Pending.

19.title: Mark Verification Pending When Steel Grade is Unknown

content.text: System shall mark verification as Pending when the steel grade is unknown.
verification marked as Pending when steel grade is unknown
the system marks the verification as Pending.
System shall display the Overall Severity Logic section within the Material Strength Verification evaluation panel.
the Overall Severity Logic section displayed
I understand how final severity is determined.
the system shows the Overall Severity Logic section.
System shall display a description indicating that the final severity is governed by the worst result among applicable strength checks.
see a description explaining how final severity is determined
the Material Strength Verification evaluation panel is open,
the Overall Severity Logic section is displayed,
the system shows a description stating that the final severity is governed by the worst result among applicable strength checks.

22.title: Display Yield Strength Check as Severity Parameter

content.text: System shall display Yield Strength Check as one of the parameters used to determine the final severity.
Yield Strength Check displayed as a severity parameter
I know it contributes to the final severity evaluation.
the Overall Severity Logic section is visible,
the system displays Yield Strength Check as one of the parameters used to determine final severity.

23.title: Display Tensile Strength Check as Severity Parameter

content.text: System shall display Tensile Strength Check (if applicable) as an additional parameter used to determine the final severity.
Tensile Strength Check displayed when applicable
I know it contributes to severity evaluation when available.
tensile strength verification data is available,
the Overall Severity Logic section is displayed,
the system shows Tensile Strength Check as an additional parameter used to determine final severity.

24.title: Determine Final Severity Using Only Yield Strength When Tensile Data is Unavailable

content.text: System shall consider only Yield Strength Check for determining final severity when tensile strength verification data is not available.
the system to evaluate final severity using only yield strength when tensile data is unavailable
evaluation remains valid even without tensile verification.
tensile strength verification data is not available,
the system considers only the Yield Strength Check.

25.title: Determine Final Severity Using Both Yield and Tensile Strength

content.text: System shall consider both Yield Strength Check and Tensile Strength Check when tensile strength verification data is available.
both yield and tensile strength checks considered when data is available
the evaluation uses all applicable parameters.
tensile strength verification data is available,
the system evaluates both Yield Strength Check and Tensile Strength Check.
System shall display the System Defined label indicating that the evaluation model cannot be edited.
see a “System Defined” label indicating the evaluation model cannot be edited
I understand configuration values are locked.
the system shows a “System Defined” label indicating that the evaluation model cannot be edited.
System shall allow the user to close the evaluation panel using the Close button or top-right close icon.
close the evaluation panel using the Close button or close icon
I can return to the testing list.
I click the Close button or the top-right close (X) icon,
the system closes the panel and returns me to the Rules and Action → Testing list.
